title: Video and sound
checkLabels.t5: It does not start on its own
why.empty: The editor is empty. You deleted the page instead of adding a video. Bring the code back.
why.not-closed: A tag is open and never closed.
hint2.not-closed: </video> must be there, even when there is nothing between the two tags.
hint3.not-closed: <video src="..." controls></video> — it opens and closes right away. The closing tag is not pointless, you will see why.
why.no-video: There is no video. The tag is <video>.
hint2.no-video: Put it after the "Watch it in action" paragraph.
hint3.no-video: <video src="film.mp4" controls></video> — the file in src, the buttons with controls.
why.no-src: The tag is there, but it does not say which file — or the src is empty. An empty video is a black rectangle that does nothing.
hint3.no-src: src is the same attribute you already use on . It works the same way.
why.no-controls: The video is there, but it has no buttons. Without controls the person stares at a still frame and cannot play it. One attribute — and the play button, the bar and the volume appear.
hint2.no-controls: controls is an attribute with no value. It simply exists or it does not.
hint3.no-controls: <video src="..." controls></video> — no quotes, no equals sign. Just the word.
hint4.no-controls: Add controls inside the opening tag, after src.
why.autoplay: You added autoplay. Think of the last time you opened a site and something suddenly started talking. What did you do? You closed the tab. You did not hunt for the stop button — you just left.
hint2.autoplay: You are making a decision for the person on the other side — about their speakers, in their room, next to their sleeping child.
hint3.autoplay: One attribute, no value — just like controls. It simply exists. It does not need ="true" to work, and it is not switched off with ="false".
hint4.autoplay: Remove autoplay. Keep controls and let the person decide.
why.wrong-path: The path does not point at the video. It is at /uroci/bike.mp4.
hint2.wrong-path: Check for a typo. And remember that capital letters matter.
blocks.0.text: The photo shows the bike. But you want to show how it moves.
blocks.3.text: Run it without controls and see what happens: a picture that does not move and will not play. The person stares and wonders.
blocks.4.text: Add controls — the play button, the bar and the sound appear. One attribute, with no value. It simply exists or it does not.
blocks.5.text: Do not play it by itself
blocks.6.text: There is an autoplay attribute. It works. Do not use it.
blocks.7.text: Think of the last time you opened a site and something suddenly started talking. What did you do? You closed the tab. You did not hunt for the stop button — you just left.
blocks.8.text: Browsers block it by default now. Not because it is a bug, but because everyone hates it.
blocks.10.text: <audio> works the same way — same attributes, same rules. It just has no picture.
blocks.11.text: controls again. No autoplay again. Especially no autoplay.
blocks.12.text: Both are block tags with a closing twin, unlike . You will see the reason in a moment.
blocks.14.text: Here is why <video> has a closing tag: between the opening and the closing one you can put things.
blocks.15.text: First — text shown if the browser cannot play the video. An old browser, a broken file. You put a link to the file itself there, so the person can at least download it.
blocks.16.text: Second — subtitles. A separate <track> tag inside, pointing at a text file. For deaf people that is the only way to know what is being said.
blocks.17.text: Third — several files in different formats, if you want it to work everywhere.
blocks.18.text: The empty space between the tags is not empty. That is where everything lives that happens when something goes wrong.
blocks.19.text: Your turn. Add the video and make it so a person can play it themselves.
